exec_summary: GrowthBook clicks grew modestly to 3,481 this week, up from 3,464 (+0.5%), while impressions jumped 10.2% to 114,423 and average position improved from 9.56 to 9.1. The homepage (/) accounts for the largest single decline at -32 clicks, but that is branded search fluctuation and not a signal of broader organic weakness. Comparison page data returned no tracked views this week, which looks like a data quality gap rather than a real drop to zero.

The most important strategic finding this week is Amplitude's aggressive push into comparison and category pages targeting startup and developer audiences. Four of the top five opportunities this week are Amplitude-owned pages covering feature flag tools for startups, mobile A/B testing for developers, product experimentation tools, and top A/B testing platforms. These are high-intent, bottom-of-funnel queries where GrowthBook has a credible story to tell but currently no dedicated content competing for them. Missing these means Amplitude is capturing evaluation-stage traffic from exactly the buyers GrowthBook should be winning.

No competitor published new content this week that is directly relevant to feature flags or experimentation. Competitor content surge data is also flat. The quieter week externally makes it a good window to close the content gaps Amplitude has been quietly building out.
content_recommendations: 1. Write a /compare or /blog page targeting 'Best feature flag tools for startups' — Amplitude owns this query unopposed with a brand-new page (KD n/a = zero competition). Startups are GrowthBook's core audience and this is a high-intent, bottom-of-funnel query.
2. Write a developer-focused guide targeting 'Best mobile A/B testing for developers' — Amplitude's comparison page targets GrowthBook's primary buyer persona. No competition currently.

3. Write a startup experimentation guide targeting 'Best product experimentation tools for startups' — Third in Amplitude's cluster. Publishing all three as a sprint builds topical authority and intercepts Amplitude's funnel at multiple entry points.

4. Refresh /compare/growthbook-vs-launchdarkly — LaunchDarkly's feature flags page (KD 38) is the top-ranked keyword opportunity. The compare page is ranking at position 8.4 with 23 clicks in 28 days — optimization here has clear upside.

5. Write a page targeting 'Top A/B testing platforms' — Amplitude is building a comparison content moat. GrowthBook needs at least one best-of page to compete in this category before the gap widens further.

opportunities.why: LaunchDarkly ranks for 'a/b rollouts' while GrowthBook has weak or no coverage. Score 83.9.
Statsig ranks for 'ab split test' while GrowthBook has weak or no coverage. Score 83.4.
